Looking at the image, I can see:
- The white diamond is still on the top dark gray diamond at [9, 49]
- The purple outline still has a dark gray diamond with purple center
- The orange circle and purple square are still merged
- The chain seems to have shifted - the orange circle and purple square are now to the right of the top dark gray diamond
Wait, actually looking more carefully, the chain has reconfigured. The orange circle and purple square are now positioned differently. The dark gray diamond at [35, 45] is still there, and the dark gray diamond at [41, 49] is still there.
Actually, I think the chain has shifted. Let me look at the image more carefully:
- Top: white diamond on dark gray diamond
- Below: orange circle and purple square (merged)
- Below: dark gray diamond with purple center
- Below: dark gray diamond with orange center

- Top: white diamond on dark gray diamond
- Below: orange circle and purple square (merged) - but they're now to the right
- Below: dark gray diamond with purple center
- Below: dark gray diamond with orange center
Actually, looking at the image, the chain seems to have shifted to the right. The orange circle and purple square are now to the right of the top dark gray diamond.
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me just check the current state and try clicking on the dark gray diamond in the purple outline.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking on purple square didn't move white diamond - it's still on top dark gray diamond [9,49]. But the chain seems to have shifted. Let me try clicking on the dark gray diamond in the purple outline at [18,57].

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current chain objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] >= 8 and n['pixels'] <= 30:
        center = None
        ccolor = None
        if n['children']:
            child = seg['nodes'][n['children'][0]]
            center = child['boundary'][0]
            ccolor = child['color']
        if n['color'] in ('W', 'G', 'O', 'p', 'M'):
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center={center}, cc={ccolor}")

# Try clicking on the dark gray diamond in the purple outline
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 57}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
